item.title: Fijian Drua remains financially stable as World Rugby funding nears end
item.description: World Rugby’s funding support for the Fijian Drua will conclude in June 2026, and the Swire Shipping Fijian Drua remains well positioned to continue operating successfully and sustainably. Drua says they have spent the past five years building a strong foundation, and preparations for the end of this funding have been underway for some time.
item.content:encoded:
World Rugby’s funding support for the Fijian Drua will conclude in June 2026, and the Swire Shipping Fijian Drua remains well positioned to continue operating successfully and sustainably.

Drua says they have spent the past five years building a strong foundation, and preparations for the end of this funding have been underway for some time.

They say that as a responsible organisation, the Fijian Drua has proactively planned for this transition.

Drua says the club is firmly established with a strong brand, a loyal fan base, and one of the strongest commercial partnership portfolios in the competition.

The club says that in a relatively short period, the Drua has grown beyond rugby to become a significant contributor to Fiji’s economy through sports tourism and broader economic activity.

The Drua say they also continue to benefit from valuable support through the Australian Government’s PacificAus Sports programme.

The club stresses that, like any successful business, they regularly review and adjust their cost base to ensure long-term sustainability.

They say as one source of revenue concludes, they are actively developing and growing alternative revenue streams, including paid streaming services and other commercial opportunities.

The Drua says they will also see considerable potential in expanding the Drua brand internationally and strengthening engagement with the Fijian diaspora.

They note that these initiatives are essential to maintaining our competitiveness in one of the toughest rugby competitions in the world.

The McDonald’s Fiji Drua Women continue to perform strongly with consistent finals appearances, and ongoing investment in their pathways will be equally important to sustaining that success.

They say they are also continuing discussions with the Government and tourism stakeholders because the Fijian Drua is more than a rugby club—it is a national asset.

The club contributes more than $108 million annually to Fiji’s economy, with its sports tourism impact continuing to grow.

They say in February 2027 alone, more than 200 rugby players and staff from Australia and Canada are expected to travel to Fiji to play against Junior, Development and Women’s teams, further demonstrating the club’s growing value to the nation.

item.title: fijivillage Straight Talk live at 7pm tonight on HIV cases and the work underway
item.description: A special fijivillage Straight Talk With Vijay Narayan focusing on HIV and the work of the National HIV Outbreak and Cluster Response Taskforce, will be held live at 7pm tonight from Fiji Showcase 2026 at the Vodafone Arena. The guests on the show will be led by the Chair of the National HIV Outbreak and Cluster Response Taskforce, Doctor Jason Mitchell.
item.content:encoded:
A special fijivillage Straight Talk With Vijay Narayan focusing on HIV and the work of the National HIV Outbreak and Cluster Response Taskforce, will be held live at 7pm tonight from Fiji Showcase 2026 at the Vodafone Arena.

The guests on the show will be led by the Chair of the National HIV Outbreak and Cluster Response Taskforce, Doctor Jason Mitchell.

You can watch the show on our facebook page, YouTube channel, TikTok and X account, fijivillage at 7pm Wednesday.

You can also catch the podcast, fijivillage Straight Talk With Vijay Narayan on Spotify and Apple Podcasts.

item.title: Legal timeframe for issuance of Writ of Elections starts today
item.description: The legal timeframe for the issuance of the Writ of Elections starts today based on the current term of parliament, and this means that the earliest date that the general elections can be held is August 7th this year. According to the law, the Prime Minister advises the President on when the Writ of Elections would be issued.
item.content:encoded:
The legal timeframe for the issuance of the Writ of Elections starts today based on the current term of parliament, and this means that the earliest date that the general elections can be held is August 7th this year.

According to the law, the Prime Minister advises the President on when the Writ of Elections would be issued.

Based on the legal timeframe, the last date for the issuance of the writ is December 24th this year.

If that happens, then the last date to have general elections is February 6th next year

When the writ is issued, voter registrations and registration of new political parties stop.

Supervisor of Elections, Ana Mataiciwa calls on all eligible voters to come forward and register as a voter if you have not done that as yet.

She says for those registering as a voter for the first time, all they have to do is bring their valid photo ID and their original or certified birth certificate.

Once you have your voter card, that card does not expire, and you do not need to get a new voter card for every election.

Mataiciwa is also urging those that may have moved and their address has changed, to update their details.

She is also urging all Fijians to not only get registered, but to go to the polling venue and vote during the general elections when it happens.

item.title: Chance Wong turns rejection into success in Japan as Beppu City world ambassador
item.description: At just 19 years old, Chance Rolano Kisoko Waqatabu Wong is already carrying Fiji’s name with pride on the international stage after being appointed Beppu City’s World Ambassador in Japan. The young Fijian, who is studying International Relations at Ritsumeikan Asia Pacific University, recently received the honour from Beppu City Hall in recognition of his efforts to connect international students
item.content:encoded:
At just 19 years old, Chance Rolano Kisoko Waqatabu Wong is already carrying Fiji’s name with pride on the international stage after being appointed Beppu City’s World Ambassador in Japan.

The young Fijian, who is studying International Relations at Ritsumeikan Asia Pacific University, recently received the honour from Beppu City Hall in recognition of his efforts to connect international students with local residents and promote cultural understanding.

For Wong, the appointment is more than a personal milestone, it is a chance to inspire other young Fijians to dream beyond their circumstances and believe that opportunities can come from the most unexpected places.

Speaking to fijivillage News, Wong, who attended Sacred Heart College in Suva, says his journey has not been without setbacks.

Wong revealed that he had applied to four of Suva’s top secondary schools but was rejected by all of them before being welcomed by Sacred Heart College, formerly known as Cathedral Secondary School.

Rather than allowing the rejection to define him, Wong says it became a turning point that helped shape his character and determination.

After high school, he pursued his dream of studying International Relations and was accepted into a university in Japan on scholarship.

Since arriving in Beppu, Wong says he has immersed himself in volunteer work and community activities, eventually helping to bridge the gap between international students and the local Japanese community.

He says one of the key moments in that journey came after meeting a staff member from Beppu City Hall, who encouraged him to get involved in volunteer programmes.

From there, Wong began bringing together fellow international students to assist in local events and activities, including school visits, sports programmes, Christmas events and the Beppu Onsen Festival.

He says the work allowed him to create meaningful links between students from overseas and local residents, while also giving him a stronger appreciation of the role young people can play in building communities.

Wong believes his passion for community work began long before he left Fiji.

He recalled growing up around community outreach through his parents’ work and spending time among street dwellers in Suva as a young child, experiences he says shaped his compassion and sense of service.

Insert: Chance - Be part of community work June 23

Those early experiences, he said, nurtured his heart for people and laid the foundation for the path he is now on.

Despite his growing achievements overseas, Wong says his long-term goal is to return home and serve Fiji.

He hopes to one day become a diplomat to Japan and play a role in strengthening ties between the two countries.

He is also working on ideas to create stronger cultural exchange opportunities between Pacific students and Japanese students, including possible collaborations between the University of the South Pacific and APU.

Wong has a simple message for young people in Fiji: do not be afraid to take chances.

He says one step towards a goal can lead to something far greater, and hopes his own story can be a reminder to young Fijians that no setback has to be the end of the journey.

As he continues his studies in Japan and takes on his new role as Beppu City’s World Ambassador, Wong says he wants to be a beacon of hope for Fiji’s youth and proof that with courage, faith and hard work, it is possible to create something beautiful out of life’s challenges.

item.description: A 40-year-old man who has been charged for allegedly sexually assaulting his 14-year-old daughter will front the Nasinu Magistrate

item.title: Lateef’s bail pending appeal application denied and dismissed

item.description: Fiji Council of Social Services Executive Director Vani Catanasiga will appear on fijivillage Her-Story with Temalesi Vono at 7pm

item.title: Police to question arresting officers in Iveri Tuimasi death investigation
item.description: The post-mortem examination report for the late Iveri Tuimasi has been provided to his next of kin and investigators from the Criminal Investigations Department. Police say the next phase of the investigation will involve interviewing all individuals involved in Tuimasi's arrest during a drug raid conducted in Dakuni, Beqa.
item.content:encoded:
The post-mortem examination report for the late Iveri Tuimasi has been provided to his next of kin and investigators from the Criminal Investigations Department.

Police say the next phase of the investigation will involve interviewing all individuals involved in Tuimasi's arrest during a drug raid conducted in Dakuni, Beqa.

They say that upon completion of the interview process, all witness statements, evidential materials and investigation reports will be compiled and submitted to the Office of the Director of Public Prosecutions (ODPP) for an independent legal assessment.
